Title: Overexpression of the homeobox-leucine zipper protein ATHB-6 improves the drought tolerance of maize (Zea mays L.)

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: A root transcriptome of the drought-tolerant line M8186 was used to initially identify drought-responsive HD-Zip I genes; ATHB-6 showed particularly strong induction and low homology to other maize HD-Zip I members, making it a distinct candidate for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ATHB-6 encodes an HD-Zip I transcription factor with a conserved HD and leucine zipper motif, and its protein is efficiently expressed and accumulates as a ~26 kDa band.
2. ATHB-6 is nuclear-localized and specifically binds the CAATAATTG inverted repeat motif, indicating a direct role in transcriptional regulation.
3. ATHB-6 expression and promoter activity are strongly induced by drought in maize roots, with promoter::GUS lines showing root-specific and time-dependent induction under 12% PEG6000.
4. Overexpression of ATHB-6 in maize increases seed germination, root elongation and adventitious/supporting root formation, and improves survival and recovery under PEG6000 and soil/field drought, whereas CRISPR knockout lines are more sensitive to drought.
5. ATHB-6 overexpression reduces ROS (H2O2 and O2−) accumulation and MDA content under drought by enhancing SOD and CAT activities, thereby alleviating oxidative damage.
6. ATHB-6 positively regulates the expression of multiple ROS-related and drought-responsive genes (ZmCAT3, ZmSOD1, ZmSOS1, ZmLTP3, ZmRD29B, ZmRD22, ZmCBF4, ZmABI4) and ABA pathway genes (NCED3, SnRK2.6, COR15, DREB2A) under drought.
7. Transgenic ATHB-6-overexpressing maize lines display improved seed setting rate, yield, and seed vigor under field drought, supporting ATHB-6 as a promising target gene for improving drought tolerance in maize.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome (RNA-seq) of the drought-tolerant maize inbred line M8186 was previously generated and used to screen drought-responsive HD-Zip I transcription factors, including ATHB-6; no public database accession number was provided.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ATHB-6 acts as a positive regulator of drought tolerance in maize, enhancing seed germination, root growth (including adventitious and supporting roots), plant survival and recovery under drought, while reducing oxidative damage and improving yield-related traits under water deficit.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: ATHB-6 is a nuclear HD-Zip I transcription factor that specifically binds the CAATAATTG inverted repeat motif in yeast one-hybrid assays and, when overexpressed in maize, enhances expression of ROS-scavenging and ABA-responsive genes, increases SOD and CAT enzyme activities, and thereby reduces ROS accumulation and oxidative damage under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ROS signaling and ABA-dependent and ABA-independent stress-response pathways under drought, as indicated by activation of ROS-scavenging genes and ABA-responsive marker genes.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmCAT3; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmSOD1; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmSOS1; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmLTP3; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmRD29B; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmRD22; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmCBF4; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> ZmABI4; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> NCED3; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> SnRK2.6; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> COR15; ATHB-6 -->(activation)--> DREB2A, based on increased transcript levels of these marker genes in ATHB-6-overexpressing lines compared with WT under 12% PEG6000-induced drought.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Homeodomain-leucine zipper I (HD-Zip I) transcription factor containing a highly conserved homeodomain (HD) and adjacent leucine zipper (LZ) motif.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, as shown by ATHB-6-GFP fusion protein localized exclusively to nuclei in tobacco epidermal cells observed by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: ATHB-6 is strongly induced in maize roots by drought: among 17 HD-Zip I genes in roots of drought-treated M8186 plants, ATHB-6 showed a marked change in expression; promoter::GUS analysis revealed root-specific GUS expression that increased progressively with longer 12% PEG6000 treatment times.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic drought stress induced by 12% (w/v) PEG6000 solution in seedlings, air-drying dehydration, and soil/field drought stress; analyses focused on responses of ATHB-6 transgenic and WT plants under these water-deficit conditions.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance, ROS homeostasis, and ABA-related stress signaling regulated by an HD-Zip I transcription factor in maize.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance), root system development (root elongation and adventitious/supporting roots), and yield-related traits under drought.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 12% PEG6000 treatment, ATHB-6-overexpressing maize seedlings showed an approximately 11% higher seed germination rate than WT; OE6 lines exhibited significantly longer primary and adventitious roots and lower MDA content under drought than WT and KO23 plants, as well as increased SOD and CAT activities and reduced H2O2 and O2− levels.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: ATHB-6 overexpression promoted adventitious root differentiation at stem nodes and supporting root development in the field, increased seed setting rate, grain size/100-seed weight, and seed vigor under drought, while knockout lines showed poorer root systems, yield, and seed vigor than WT under stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Maize inbred lines M8186 (drought-tolerant) and H8186 (drought-sensitive, WT background); T3 transgenic maize lines overexpressing ATHB-6 (OE6-1, OE6-2, OE6-3) under the CaMV35S promoter; T3 CRISPR/Cas9 ATHB-6 knockout lines (KO23-1, KO23-2, KO23-3) under the U6-1 promoter; maize ATHB-6 promoter::GUS transgenic maize; tobacco (Nicotiana tabacum cv. NC89) leaves for transient GFP localization; E. coli BL21 cells for ATHB-6 protein expression; Agrobacterium tumefaciens EHA105 for plant transformation.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Inbred lines H8186 (drought-sensitive, WT background for transgenics) and M8186 (drought-tolerant, used for root transcriptome and expression analyses)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Bioinformatic identification and phylogenetic analysis of HD-Zip I genes; RNA-seq-based root transcriptome screening; qRT-PCR analysis of gene expression; cloning of ATHB-6 CDS and promoter; construction of overexpression (CaMV35S::ATHB-6), promoter::GUS (pATHB-6::GUS), and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout vectors; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of maize and tobacco; selection of T3 homozygous transgenic lines; SDS-PAGE and Western blotting of ATHB-6 in E. coli; subcellular localization by ATHB-6-GFP fusion and confocal microscopy; yeast one-hybrid assays to test binding to CAATAATTG motif; PEG6000-induced osmotic drought and soil/field drought treatments; seed germination and root growth assays; DAB staining for ROS, measurements of H2O2, O2−, MDA, proline, SOD and CAT activities; GUS histochemical staining and ELISA for GUS protein; field evaluation of agronomic traits under drought; statistical analysis by one-way ANOVA.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation was achieved by generating maize lines overexpressing ATHB-6 under the CaMV35S promoter and CRISPR/Cas9 ATHB-6 knockout lines, then comparing seed germination, root growth, plant wilting and recovery, and yield-related traits under PEG6000-induced and soil/field drought; subcellular localization of ATHB-6-GFP in tobacco nuclei; yeast one-hybrid assays for DNA-binding specificity to CAATAATTG; DAB staining and biochemical assays for ROS (H2O2, O2−), MDA, proline, SOD and CAT activities; and qRT-PCR analyses of ROS-related, drought-responsive, and ABA pathway marker genes in WT, OE6, and KO23 plants.
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: T3 ATHB-6-overexpression (OE6) and ATHB-6-knockout (KO23) lines and WT plants were planted in the genetically modified crop test base of Jilin Agricultural University (Changchun, China) in 2021 under natural field conditions with drought stress; a randomized block design with three replicates was used, and agronomic traits including plant height, supporting root development, seed setting rate, yield, and seed vigor were evaluated.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: HD-Zip I family members were identified genome-wide using HMM search of the HD domain against the maize genome and Pfam, then 17 HD-Zip I genes were catalogued; drought-induced expression in roots of the drought-tolerant line M8186 was profiled and ATHB-6 was selected based on strong induction; ATHB-6 coding sequence and promoter were cloned by PCR from maize for vector construction.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of ATHB-6 in maize enhances drought tolerance in greenhouse and field, improving root system architecture, supporting root development, seed setting rate, yield, and seed vigor under drought; thus ATHB-6 is proposed as a crucial target gene for breeding or engineering drought-tolerant maize cultivars.
